title: UC Berkeley Organ-on-a-Chip Replicates 40 Years of Aging in 4 Days
description: Researchers used a miniaturized organ-on-a-chip to mimic 40 years of aging in human fat and liver tissue in just four days. Oxytocin showed the best rejuvenative effect; rapamycin showed almost none.

title: UT Health San Antonio Launches Multi-Phase Rapamycin Aging Trial
description: Funded by the NIA, this randomized placebo-controlled trial will enroll 84 older adults to study rapamycin dosing, safety, and biological markers for healthy aging.

title: NIH ITP March 2026 Results: 11 Compounds Tested, Zero Extended Lifespan
description: The NIA Interventions Testing Program released March 2026 results: alpha-ketoglutarate, astaxanthin, meclizine, and 8 other compounds all failed to extend mouse lifespan. Pioglitazone shortened female lifespan by 6.2%.

title: The Business of Longevity: Turning Aging into an Investable Market
description: Southern California biotech labs, healthtech startups, and venture firms are converging on aging as a programmable, measurable, and investable frontier.

title: Biotech Stocks and Longevity Investing: Trends to Track in 2026
description: Biotech surged in 2025 with longevity investment doubling to $8.49B. The FDA approved 46 new drugs in 2025, including the first class labeled potential longevity therapeutics.

title: Longevity Innovation Forum: How Should Capital Think About Longevity Now
description: Panelists at the San Diego Longevity Innovation Forum discussed therapeutic aging biology vs. consumer longevity, stressing that breakthroughs will come from targeting aging biology itself.

title: Anne Brunet on the Science of Aging and Longevity Research Frontiers
description: Stanford geneticist Anne Brunet discusses what aging really is, how lifestyle choices influence longevity, and the promising frontiers of aging research.

title: 11 Anti-Aging Biotech Companies Leading Longevity in 2026
description: Profiles of top longevity biotechs including Altos Labs ($3B), Cambrian ($30.8M ARPA-H award), and Genflow Biosciences, all advancing reprogramming and gene therapy approaches.

title: Vitamin C Inhibits ACSL4 to Alleviate Ferro-Aging in Primates
description: Steve Horvath notes March 2026 as a strong month for longevity supplement research, citing new data on vitamin C and ferro-aging alongside the COSMOS multivitamin trial.

title: Wilbe Longevity Build Week: Accelerating Aging Science to Ventures
description: A global online sprint for researchers, engineers, and builders exploring how longevity science -- from cellular reprogramming to epigenetic clocks -- can become real ventures.

title: Longevity, Cancer, and the Question Oncologists Get Wrong
description: An oncologist examines how Attia, Sinclair, and Topol each approach longevity differently, arguing cancer survivorship requires both life extension and preservation of strength and function.

title: Daily Multivitamins Slow Biological Aging in COSMOS Trial
description: Two years of daily multivitamin use slowed all five biological aging clocks by about 4 months. Seniors with accelerated aging at baseline benefited most.

title: Biohacking Becomes the New Anti-Aging: David Sinclair's Influence
description: Harvard genetics professor David Sinclair's review of OneSkin led to a significant sales boost, illustrating the growing crossover between longevity science and consumer wellness.

title: US Government Funds $144M in Aging Clinical Trials via ARPA-H PROSPR
description: ARPA-H announced $144M for the PROSPR program -- the first major human clinical trials targeting aging directly, including rapamycin-based approaches and censenuvudine.

title: Investors Eye $420B Anti-Aging Market Milestone
description: The anti-aging therapy market is targeting a $420 billion milestone as investor interest accelerates across therapeutics, diagnostics, and wellness platforms.

title: Polyamines: Anti-Aging Compound May Also Fuel Cancer via eIF5A2
description: Natural polyamines boost healthy cells via eIF5A1 but turbocharge cancer via nearly identical protein eIF5A2. Findings suggest targeting eIF5A2 could slow cancer without harming anti-aging benefits.

title: Rejuvenation Roundup February 2026: Senolytic Effectiveness via Mitochondrial Stress
description: Monthly roundup covers mitochondrial stress boosting senolytic effectiveness (Nature Aging), NK cell restoration, AI diagnostic tools, and SIRT6's role in brain protection.

title: Rapamycin: The Dimmer Switch Dilemma -- Evidence Review for 2026
description: Comprehensive analysis of 500+ papers finds zero qualifying studies of long-term oral rapamycin in healthy humans with validated aging biomarkers. Safe short-term, but evidence for slowing aging remains preliminary.

title: Brown University Wins $22M ARPA-H Award to Study Aging with Censavudine
description: A large clinical trial will enroll 200+ healthy adults aged 60-65, testing whether the HIV drug Censavudine can slow human aging by reducing chronic inflammation and interferon signaling.

title: Senolytics Clear Up to 70% of Zombie Cells; New Senosensitizer Class Proposed
description: Cedars-Sinai study found senolytics eliminate 30-70% of senescent cells. Remaining resistant cells can be activated by infections, leading to a proposed new drug class called senosensitizers.

title: Shingles Vaccine Drastically Cuts Cardiac Events in Atherosclerotic Patients
source: Fight Aging / American College of Cardiology
description: Analysis of 123,411 patients found Shingrix/Zostavax reduced major cardiac events by 46%, all-cause mortality by 66%, and heart attacks by 32% in those with atherosclerosis.

title: Thymic Health Predicts Longevity: Deep Learning Analysis of 27,000+ Subjects
description: Deep learning quantification of thymic atrophy in 25,031 (NLST) and 2,581 (Framingham) subjects showed thymic health correlates strongly with mortality and age-related disease outcomes.

title: Sleep EEG Brain Age Index Predicts Dementia Risk: 39% Higher per Decade
description: Machine learning on sleep EEG from 7,105 participants across five cohorts found a 10-year brain age index increase linked to 39% higher dementia risk.

title: Genes Influence Human Lifespan Far More Than Thought: Over 50% Heritable
description: Weizmann Institute researchers found that heritability contributes over 50% of lifespan when extrinsic mortality is stripped out, more than doubling previous estimates of 20-25%.

title: Rapamycin Protects Immune Cells Against DNA Damage in Aging
description: New findings explain why rapamycin and mTOR inhibitors have anti-aging potential in the immune system: they improve DNA stability, a central driver of immune aging.
